HotReloadCheck};\n41\t\n42\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n43\t// Global shutdown flag for signal handlers\n44\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n45\t\n46\t/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n47\t/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n48\t/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n49\t#[cfg(unix)]\n50\tstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n51\t\n52\t/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n53\t/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n54\t#[cfg(unix)]\n55\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n56\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\t/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n60\t/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n61\t#[cfg(unix)]\n62\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n63\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n64\t    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n68\t/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n69\t#[cfg(unix)]\n70\tstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n71\t\n72\t/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n73\t///\n74\t/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n75\t/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n76\t/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n77\t#[cfg(unix)]\n78\textern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n79\t    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n80\t    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n81\t    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n82\t    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n83\t    if !ptr.is_null() {\n84\t        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n85\t        // for the entire program duration.\n86\t        unsafe {\n87\t            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n88\t        }\n89\t        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n90\t        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n95\t///\n96\t/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n97\t/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n98\t/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n99\t#[cfg(unix)]\n100\tunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n101\t    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n102\t\n103\t    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n104\t    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n105\t    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n106\t    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n107\t    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n108\t    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n109\t    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n110\t\n111\t    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n112\t    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n113\t    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n114\t    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n115\t        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n116\t        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n117\t            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n118\t        } else {\n119\t            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n120\t            tracing::warn!(\n121\t                signal = sig,\n122\t                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n123\t                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n124\t            );\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n130\t/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n131\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n132\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n133\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n137\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n138\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n142\t///\n143\t/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n144\t/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\n145\tconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n146\t\n147\t/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n148\t///\n149\t/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n150\t/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n151\t/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\n152\tconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n153\t\n154\t/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\n155\tpub struct Worker {\n156\t    config: Config,\n157\t    worker_name: String,\n158\t    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n159\t    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n160\t    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n161\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n162\t    strands: StrandRunner,\n163\t    claimer: Claimer,\n164\t    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n165\t    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n166\t    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n167\t    health: HealthMonitor,\n168\t    registry: Registry,\n169\t    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n170\t    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n171\t\n172\t    // State machine fields\n173\t    state: WorkerState,\n174\t    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n175\t    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n176\t    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n177\t    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n178\t    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n179\t    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n180\t    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n181\t    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n182\t    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n183\t    retry_count: u32,\n184\t    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n185\t    beads_processed: u64,\n186\t    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n187\t    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n188\t    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n189\t\n190\t    // /// visible in the JSONL log.\n226\t    pub fn new_with_telemetry(\n227\t        config: Config,\n228\t        worker_name: String,\n229\t        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n230\t        telemetry: implementation.\n236\t    ///\n237\t    /// Creates its own telemetry instance. Prefer [`new_with_telemetry`] when\n238\t    /// the caller has already created and started telemetry for early boot\n239\t    /// diagnostics.\n240\t    pub fn new(config: Config, worker_name: String, store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>) -> Self {\n241\t        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n242\t        let telemetry = Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry)\n243\t            .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n244\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, Worker loop: the core NEEDLE state machine.\n//!\n//! The Worker executes the strand waterfall, claims beads, dispatches the\n//! agent, handles outcomes, and emits telemetry for every transition.\n//!\n//! State transitions are explicit — there is no implicit fallthrough and no\n//! state that does not have a defined handler. The worker emits telemetry for\n//! every transition.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `strand`, `claim`, `prompt`, `dispatch`, `outcome`,\n//!             `bead_store`, `telemetry`, `health`, `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\nuse anyhow::{bail, Context, Result};\n\n#[cfg(unix)]\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, AtomicUsize, Ordering as AtomicOrdering};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::canary::CanaryRunner;\nuse crate::claim::Claimer;\nuse crate::commit_hook;\nuse crate::config::{Config, ConfigLoader};\nuse crate::cost::{self, BudgetCheck, EffortData};\nuse crate::dispatch::{self, Dispatcher};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::mitosis::MitosisEvaluator;\nuse crate::outcome::OutcomeHandler;\nuse crate::prompt::{BuiltPrompt, PromptBuilder};\nuse crate::rate_limit::RateLimiter;\nuse crate::registry::{Registry, WorkerEntry};\nuse crate::strand::StrandRunner;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::types::{AgentOutcome, Bead, BeadId, ClaimResult, IdleAction, Outcome, WorkerState};\nuse crate::upgrade::{self, HotReloadCheck};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Global shutdown flag for signal handlers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n\n/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n\n/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nextern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n    if !ptr.is_null() {\n        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n        // for the entire program duration.\n        unsafe {\n            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n        }\n        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n\n    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n\n    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n        } else {\n            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n            tracing::warn!(\n                signal = sig,\n                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n///\n/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\nconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n\n/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n///\n/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\nconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n\n/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\npub struct Worker {\n    config: Config,\n    worker_name: String,\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    strands: StrandRunner,\n    claimer: Claimer,\n    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n    health: HealthMonitor,\n    registry: Registry,\n    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n\n    // State machine fields\n    state: WorkerState,\n    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    retry_count: u32,\n    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n\n    // Transient fields — pass data between state handlers within a single cycle.\n    built_prompt: Option<BuiltPrompt>,\n    current_strand: Option<String>,\n    exec_output: Option<(AgentOutcome, bool)>,\n    /// Effort tracking data for the current bead cycle.\n    last_effort: Option<EffortData>,\n    /// HEAD SHA captured just before agent dispatch; used to detect new commits.\n    pre_dispatch_head: Option<String>,\n    /// The workspace of the current bead store — updated when switching to remote.\n    /// Used to ensure heartbeat reports the actual workspace where work is happening.\n    current_workspace: PathBuf,\n    /// Whether `worker.booting` was already emitted externally (e.g., from CLI layer).\n    /// When true, `run()` skips emitting the booting event to avoid duplicates.\n    booting_emitted: bool,\n    /// Waterfall restart count from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n    last_waterfall_restarts: u32,\n    /// Names of strands that triggered waterfall restarts in the most recent cycle.\n    last_restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n    /// Strand evaluations from the most recent select cycle (for exhausted telemetry).\n    last_strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n    /// Timestamp when the worker entered HANDLING state.\n    /// Used by the watchdog to detect stuck HANDLING state.\n    handling_state_entered_at: Option<Instant>,\n    /// Flag set by the watchdog thread when HANDLING state timeout is detected.\n    /// The main worker loop checks this flag and forces recovery if set.\n    watchdog_triggered: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    /// Handle to the watchdog thread for cleanup on worker drop.\n    watchdog_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,\n}\n\nimpl Worker {\n    /// Construct a worker using a pre-existing telemetry instance.\n    ///\n    /// Use this when telemetry has already been started (e.g. after emitting\n    /// `worker.booting` from the CLI layer) so that early init steps are\n    /// visible in the JSONL log.\n    pub fn new_with_telemetry(\n        config: Config,\n        worker_name: String,\n        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, true)\n    }\n\n    /// Construct a worker from config, a worker name, and a bead store implementation.\n    ///\n    /// Creates its own telemetry instance. Prefer [`new_with_telemetry`] when\n    /// the caller has already created and started telemetry for early boot\n    /// diagnostics.\n    pub fn new(config: Config, worker_name: String, store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>) -> Self {\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n                Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n            });\n        Self::build(config, worker_name, store, telemetry, false)\n    }\n\n    /// Shared construction logic used by both [`new`] and [`new_with_telemetry`].\n    fn build(\n        config: Config,\n        worker_name: String,\n        store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n        booting_emitted: bool,\n    ) std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            config.worker.max_claim_retries,\n            100,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n        let prompt_builder = workspace learnings, using default prompt builder\");\n            PromptBuilder::new(&config.prompt)\n        })\n        .with_cross_workspace_skills(\n            &config.strands.explore.workspaces,\n            &config.workspace.labels,\n        )\n        .with_global_learnings(&config.strands.learning.global_learnings_file);\n        let dispatcher = match Dispatcher::new(&config, telemetry.clone()) {\n            Ok(d) => d,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to load adapters, using built-in defaults\");\n Structured telemetry — JSONL event stream, never on stdout/stderr.\n//!\n//! Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits a typed\n//! event. The emitter is non-blocking: events are queued and written by a\n//! background task. A broken sink never blocks or panics the worker.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//! ```text\n//! worker → emit() → mpsc::Sender → [background task] → TelemetryBus → Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\nuse crate::config::{ColorMode, HookConfig, StdoutFormat, StdoutSinkConfig, TelemetryConfig};\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerId, WorkerState};\n\n// ─── TelemetryEvent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single structured telemetry record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\n    /// UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Discriminant string, e.g. `\"state_transition\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// The worker that emitted this event.\n    pub worker_id: WorkerId,\n    /// Session identifier (8 hex chars, unique per worker boot).\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// Monotonically increasing counter within the session.\n    pub sequence: u64,\n    /// Bead context when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n    /// Workspace directory when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    /// Event-specific payload as a JSON value.\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Optional duration in milliseconds for timed operations.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// W3C trace-id hex (32 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub trace_id: Option<String>,\n    /// W3C span-id hex (16 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub span_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ─── EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Typed event variants emitted by all NEEDLE components.\n///\n/// Every variant maps to a `TelemetryEvent` with `event_type` matching\n/// the snake_case discriminant name.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum EventKind {\n    // ── Worker lifecycle ──\n    WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStarted {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStopped {\n        reason: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerErrored {\n        error_type: String,\n        error_message: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerExhausted {\n        cycle_count: u64,\n        last_strand: String,\n        /// How many times the waterfall restarted before exhausting.\n        waterfall_restarts: u32,\n        /// Name of each strand that returned WorkCreated (one entry    /// be processed (e.g., for `worker.booting` as the first JSONL event).\n    ///\n    /// Must be called from within a tokio runtime context.\n    pub async fn start_and_wait(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let pending = self.pending_writer.lock().unwrap().take();\n        if let Some(pw) = pending {\n            let (ready_tx, ready_rx) = tokio::sync::oneshot::channel();\n            let handle = Self::spawn_writer(pw.receiver, pw.sinks, Some(ready_tx));\n            *self.writer_handle.lock().unwrap() = Some(handle);\n\n            // Wait for the writer thread to signal it's ready.\n            // Use a timeout to avoid hanging if the thread fails to start.\n            tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5), ready_rx)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!(\"writer thread failed to start within 5s\"))?\n                .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!(\"writer thread closed before signaling ready\"))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush and shut down the background writer.\n    ///\n    /// Drops the shared sender (closing the channel) so the writer thread\n    /// processes all buffered events and flushes its `BufWriter` before\n    /// exiting. Joins the thread to guarantee completion.\n    ///\n    /// Call this at every terminal path in the worker before the tokio\n    /// Runtime is dropped, or the BufWriter flush will be cancelled.\n    pub async fn shutdown(&self) {\n        // Drop the sender to signal EOF to the writer thread.\n        *self.sender.lock().unwrap() = None;\n        // Join the writer thread so the flush completes before we return.\n        let handle = self.writer_handle.lock().unwrap().take();\n        if let Some(h) = handle {\n            let _ = h.join();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn background writer task draining the channel to all registered sinks.\n    ///\n    /// Spawns a dedicated thread with its own tokio runtime so the writer task\n    /// can immediately start processing messages, even before the main runtime's\n    /// `block_on()` is called. This fixes a deadlock where `force_flush()` would\n    /// wait indefinitely for a response from a task that hasn't started yet.\n    ///\n    /// If `ready_signal` is provided, sends () through it when the writer is\n    /// ready to process events.\n    fn spawn_writer(\n        receiver: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<WriterMessage>,\n        sinks: Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>,\n        ready_signal: Option<tokio::sync::oneshot::Sender<()>>,\n    ) -> std::thread::JoinHandle<()> {\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n                Ok(r) => r,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Fallback to stderr when tracing may not be initialized yet\n                    eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry writer thread: FAILED to create tokio runtime: {}\", e);\n                    tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to create writer runtime\");\n                    // Signal that we failed (by dropping the sender without sending)\n                    drop(ready_signal);\n                    return;\n                }\n            };\n            eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry writer thread: started successfully\");\n            if sinks.is_empty() {\n                eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry writer thread: WARNING - no sinks configured, events will be discarded!\");\n            }\n            let mut receiver = receiver;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                // Signal that the writer is ready to process events.\n                // Do this before entering the recv() loop so the caller\n                // can proceed as soon as we're ready.\n                if let Some(tx) = ready_signal {\n                    tx.send(()).ok();\n                }\n\n                let deadline = std::time::Duration::from_secs(5);\n                let mut event_count = 0u64;\n                while let Some(msg) = receiver.recv().await {\n                    match msg {\n                        WriterMessage::Event(event) => {\n                            event_count += 1;\n                            for sink in &sinks {\n                                if let Err(e) = sink.accept(&event) {\n                                    eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry: sink accept failed for event {}: {}\", event.event_type, e);\n                                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"telemetry sink accept failed\");\n                                }\n                            }\n                            if event_count == 1 {\n                                eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry writer thread: first event written: {}\", event.event_type);\n                            }\n                        }\n                        WriterMessage::Flush(reply) => {\n                            for sink in &sinks {\n                                if let Err(e) = sink.flush(deadline) {\n                                    eprintln!(\"NEEDLE telemetry: sink flush failed: {}\", e);\n                                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"telemetry sink flush on demand failed\");\n                                }\n                            }","numLines":100,"startLine":2455,"totalLines":4638}}}

Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry).unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n628\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n629\t            Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n630\t        });\n631\t    // Wait for the writer thread to be ready before emitting events.\n632\t    // This fixes a race condition where emit() would send to the channel\n633\t    // before the writer thread started processing, causing force_flush to timeout.\n634\t    rt.block_on(telemetry.start_and_wait())\n635\t        .context(\"writer thread failed to start\")?;\n636\t    telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBooting {\n637\t        worker_name: worker_name.clone(),\n638\t        version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n639\t    })?;\n640\t    // Force-flush worker.booting to disk immediately so we get a trace even if\n641\t    // subsequent init steps block indefinitely (e.g., db lock, network call).\n642\t   ))\n659\t    })?;\n660\t\n661\t    // Boot timeout guard: self-abort if init took >60 s.\n662\t    let elapsed_ms = boot_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n663\t    if elapsed_ms > 60_000 {\n664\t        telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { elapsed_ms })?;\n665\t        bail!(\"boot exceeded 60 s ({elapsed_ms} ms), aborting\");\n666\t    }\n667\t\n668\t    let result = initialization step.\n675\t///\n676\t/// Each step's completion is force-flushed to disk so that if a subsequent\n677\t/// step blocks indefinitely, the telemetry log shows exactly where it stopped.\n678\tfn init_step<T, F>(name: &str, tel: &Telemetry, f: F) -> Result<T>\n679\twhere\n680\t    F: FnOnce() -> Result<T>,\n681\t{\n682\t    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n683\t        step: name.to_string(),\n684\t    })?;\n685\t    let t = Instant::now();\n686\t    let result = f();\n687\t    let elapsed = t.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n688\t    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n689\t        step: name.to_string(),\n690\t        duration_ms: elapsed,\n691\t    })?;\n692\t    // Force-flush so the step completion is visible before the next (potentially blocking) step.\n693\t    tel.force_flush(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))?;\n694\t    result\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t/// Returns true when this process is a re-entrant inner run_worker(config: Config, worker_name: String) -> Result<()> {\n    let boot_start = Instant::now();\n    let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n\n    // Phase 0: create tokio runtime + telemetry, emit worker.booting immediately.\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().context(\"failed to create tokio runtime\")?;\n    let telemetry =\n        Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry).unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n            Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n        });\n    // Wait for the writer thread to be ready before emitting events.\n    // This fixes a race condition where emit() would send to the channel\n    // before the writer thread started processing, causing force_flush to timeout.\n    rt.block_on(telemetry.start_and_wait())\n        .context(\"writer thread failed to start\")?;\n    telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: worker_name.clone(),\n        version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n    })?;\n    // Force-flush worker.booting to disk immediately so we get a trace even if\n    // subsequent init steps block indefinitely (e.g., db lock, network call).\n    telemetry.force_flush(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))?;\n\n    // Phase 1: bead store discovery.\n    let store: Arc<dyn crate::bead_store::BeadStore> =\n        Arc::new(init_step(\"bead_store_discover\", &telemetry, || {\n            BrCliBeadStore::discover(config.workspace.default.clone())\n                .context(\"failed to locate br CLI for bead store\")\n        })?);\n\n    // Phase 2: worker construction (heavy — prompt loading, adapter discovery, etc.).\n    let mut worker = init_step(\"worker_construction\", &telemetry, || {\n        Ok(Worker::new_with_telemetry(\n            config,\n            worker_name.clone(),\n            store,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        ))\n    })?;\n\n    // Boot timeout guard: self-abort if init took >60 s.\n    let elapsed_ms = boot_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n    if elapsed_ms > 60_000 {\n        telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { elapsed_ms })?;\n        bail!(\"boot exceeded 60 s ({elapsed_ms} ms), aborting\");\n    }\n\n    let result = rt.block_on(worker.run())?;\n\n    tracing::info!(final_state = %result, \"worker finished\");\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Emit start/complete telemetry around a fallible initialization step.\n///\n/// Each step's completion is force-flushed to disk so that if a subsequent\n/// step blocks indefinitely, the telemetry log shows exactly where it stopped.\nfn init_step<T, F>(name: &str, tel: &Telemetry, f: F) -> Result<T>\nwhere\n    F: FnOnce() -> Result<T>,\n{\n    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n        step: name.to_string(),\n    })?;\n    let t = Instant::now();\n    let result = f();\n    let elapsed = t.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n        step: name.to_string(),\n        duration_ms: elapsed,\n    })?;\n    // Force-flush so the step completion is visible before the next (potentially blocking) step.\n    tel.force_flush(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))?;\n    result\n}\n\n/// Returns true when this process is a re-entrant inner invocation launched\n/// by `launch_in_tmux()`, indicated by `NEEDLE_INNER=1` in the environment.\nfn is_needle_inner() -> bool {","numLines":80,"startLine":620,"totalLines":4000}}}

The existing code does have `worker.booting` and init step telemetry, but the problem is that when something blocks during the pre-boot phase (before `worker.booting` is written), we have zero diagnostic information.\n\nThe key issue is that `worker.booting` is only emitted *after* `start_and_wait()` completes. If `start_and_wait()` blocks (e.g., waiting for the writer thread to start), we never get any telemetry output.\n\nThe fix should add `eprintln!` diagnostics at critical points before and after telemetry operations. This way, even if telemetry hangs, we can see from stderr where the process is stuck.\n\nLet me add these diagnostics:\n\n1. Before creating tokio runtime\n2. After creating tokio runtime\n3. Before creating telemetry\n4. After creating telemetry\n5. Before starting writer thread\n6. After starting writer thread\n7. Before emitting worker.booting\n8. After emitting worker.booting\n9. After force_flush\n\nThis will allow us to pinpoint exactly where the hang occurs.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a7eeacd6-79a0-4a7f-9c88-53746914337e","uuid":"d9d34a66-7272-494f-862a-e34b10cdcaad"}

CLI layer — parses commands and manages worker sessions.\n//!\n//! Entry point for the `needle` binary. Routes subcommands to worker\n//! lifecycle management. Always creates dedicated tmux sessions for workers.\n//! Re-entrant inner invocations (launched by `launch_in_tmux()`) are\n//! detected via the `NEEDLE_INNER=1` environment variable and run the\n//! worker directly without spawning another session.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `worker`, `config`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::process::Command as ProcessCommand;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse anyhow::{bail, Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse clap::{Parser, Subcommand, ValueEnum};\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, BrCliBeadStore};\nuse crate::config::{CliOverrides, Config, ConfigLoader, StdoutSinkConfig};\nuse crate::dispatch;\nuse crate::health::{HealthMonitor, HeartbeatData};\nuse crate::registry::{Registry, WorkerEntry};\nuse crate::telemetry::{self, EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::worker::Worker;\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// NATO alphabet for worker identifiers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst NATO_ALPHABET: &[&str] = &[\n    \"alpha\", \"bravo\", \"charlie\", \"delta\", \"echo\", \"foxtrot\", \"golf\", ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// NEEDLE — Navigates Every Enqueued Deliverable, Logs Effort.\n///\n/// Deterministic bead processing with explicit outcome paths.\n#[derive(Debug, Parser)]\n#[command(name = \"needle\", version, about, long_about = None)]\npub struct Cli {\n    #[command(subcommand)]\n    pub command: CliCommand,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Subcommand)]\npub enum CliCommand {\n    /// Launch worker(s) to process beads.\n    Run {\n        /// Workspace to process beads from.\n        #[arg(short = 'w', long)]\n        workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n\n        /// Agent adapter to use.\n        #[arg(short = 'a', long)]\n        agent: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Number of workers to launch.\n        #[arg(short = 'c', long, default_value = \"1\")]\n        count: u32,\n\n        /// Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming).\n        #[arg(short = 'i', long)]\n        identifier: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Agent execution timeout in seconds.\n        #[arg(short = 't', long)]\n        timeout: Option<u64>,\n\n        /// Resume an existing worker session (used by hot-reload).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        resume: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Stop running worker(s).\n    Stop {\n        /// Stop all needle workers.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        all: bool,\n\n        /// Identifier of the worker to stop.\n        #[arg(short = 'i', long)]\n        identifier: Option<String>,\n    },\n\n    /// List active workers.\n    List {\n        /// Output format.\n        #[arg(long, value_enum, default_value = \"table\")]\n        format: ListFormat,\n    },\n\n    /// Attach to a worker's tmux session.\n    Attach {\n        /// Worker identifier (e.g., alpha, bravo) or partial session name.\n        identifier: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Show fleet status, bead counts, and cost summary.\n    Status {\n        /// Output format.\n        #[arg(long, value_enum, default_value = \"table\")]\n        format: ListFormat,\n\n        /// Show per-worker breakdown.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        by_worker: bool,\n\n        /// Show cost summary with per-worker and per-workspace breakdowns.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        cost: bool,\n\n        /// Filter events since this time (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d, 2026-03-20).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        since: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Filter events until this time (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d, 2026-03-20T15:00:00Z).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        until: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Show cooldown state for idle-time strands (reflect, weave, pulse, unravel).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        idle_strands: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// View and query telemetry logs.\n    Logs {\n        /// Stream events in real-time (tail -f equivalent).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        follow: bool,\n\n        /// Filter expression(s). Supports:\n        ///   field=value    — exact match (e.g., event_type=bead.claim.succeeded)\n        ///   field~pattern  — regex match (e.g., event_type~bead\\..*)\n        ///   field>number   — numeric greater-than (e.g., duration_ms>500)\n        ///   glob           — glob on event_type (e.g., bead.claim.*)\n        /// Multiple --filter flags are ANDed together.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        filter: Vec<String>,\n\n        /// Show events since this time (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d, 2026-03-20).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        since: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Show events until this time format: LogFormat,\n    },\n\n    /// View or inspect configuration.\n    #[command(name = \"config\")]\n    ConfigCmd {\n        /// Get a specific config key.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        get: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Dump all resolved config values.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        dump: bool,\n\n        /// Show source annotations (requires --dump).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        show_source: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Check system health and repair.\n    Doctor {\n        /// Attempt automatic repair of issues found.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        repair: bool,\n\n        /// Workspace to check (defaults to config workspace).\n        #[arg(short = 'w', long)]\n        workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    },\n\n    /// Show version information.\n    Version,\n\n    /// Validate an agent adapter.\n    TestAgent {\n        /// Name of the adapter to test.\n        name: String,\n    },\n\n    /// Run canary tests against a :testing binary.\n    Canary {\n        /// Show channel status instead of running tests.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        status: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Check for and install updates from GitHub releases.\n    Upgrade {\n        /// Check only — show available update without installing.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        check: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Rollback to the previous :stable binary.\n    Rollback,\n\n    /// Run learning consolidation on demand.\n    ///\n    /// Reads bead close bodies since the last consolidation, extracts\n    /// retrospective patterns, merges them into learnings.md, and promotes\n    /// high-frequency learnings to skill files.\n    Reflect {\n        /// Workspace to consolidate (defaults to config workspace).\n        #[arg(short = 'w', long)]\n        workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n\n        /// Skip cooldown and minimum bead threshold checks.\n        #[arg(long)]\n        force: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Fetch the latest gitleaks rules and update the vendored config.\n    ///\n    /// Downloads gitleaks.toml from the upstream GitHub repository, validates\n    /// it by compiling all rules, and writes it to the output path.\n    /// Rebuild needle after running this command to embed the new rules.\n    #[command(name = \"update-rules\")]\n    UpdateRules {\n        /// Destination path for the downloaded config (default: config/gitleaks.toml).\n        #[arg(short = 'o', long)]\n        output: Option<PathBuf>,\n    },\n\n    /// Show outcome statistics aggregated from telemetry logs.\n    ///\n    /// Reads JSONL telemetry files, correlates dispatch/outcome/effort events\n    /// by bead ID, and prints per-group statistics.\n    ///\n    /// Examples:\n    ///   needle stats --by template_version --since 7d\n    ///   needle stats --by task_type --since 30d\n    ///   needle stats --by worker --since 7d --format json\n    Stats {\n        /// Dimension to group results by.\n        #[arg(long, value_enum)]\n        by: StatsBy,\n\n        /// Include only events since this time (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d, 2026-03-20).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        since: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Include only events until this time (e.g., 1h, 24h, 7d, 2026-03-20T15:00:00Z).\n        #[arg(long)]\n        until: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Output format.\n        #[arg(long, value_enum, default_value = \"table\")]\n        format: ListFormat,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Output format for the list command.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, ValueEnum)]\npub enum ListFormat {\n    Table,\n    Json,\n}\n\n/// Output format for the logs command.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, ValueEnum)]\npub enum LogFormat {\n    /// Human-readable table format (default).\n    Table,\n    /// JSON Lines format (one JSON object per line).\n    Json,\n    /// Alias for table (human-readable).\n    Human,\n    /// Alias for json (JSON Lines).\n    Jsonl,\n}\n\n/// Grouping dimension for the `needle stats` command.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, ValueEnum)]\npub enum StatsBy {\n    /// Group by template version tag (e.g., `\"pluck-v2\"`).\n    #[value(name = \"template_version\")]\n    TemplateVersion,\n    /// Group by template name / task type (e.g., `\"pluck\"`).\n    #[value(name = \"task_type\")]\n    TaskType,\n    /// Group by worker identifier (e.g., `\"needle-alpha\"`).\n    #[value(name = \"worker\")]\n    Worker,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Entry point\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Entry point called from `main`.\npub fn run() -> Result<()> {\n    let cli = Cli::parse();\n\n    match cli.command {\n        CliCommand::Run {\n            workspace,\n            agent,\n            count,\n            identifier,\n            timeout,\n            resume,\n        } => cmd_run(workspace, agent, count, identifier, timeout, resume),\n        CliCommand::Stop { all, identifier } => cmd_stop(all, identifier),\n        CliCommand::List { format } => cmd_list(format),\n        CliCommand::Attach { identifier } => cmd_attach(&identifier),\n        CliCommand::Status {\n            format,\n            by_worker,\n            cost,\n            since,\n            until,\n            idle_strands,\n        } => cmd_status(format, by_worker, cost, since, until, idle_strands),\n        CliCommand::Logs {\n            follow,\n            filter,\n            since,\n            until,\n            format,\n        } => cmd_logs(follow, filter, since, until, format),\n        CliCommand::ConfigCmd {\n            get,\n            dump,\n            show_source,\n        } => cmd_config(get, dump, show_source),\n        CliCommand::Doctor { repair, workspace } => cmd_doctor(repair, workspace),\n        CliCommand::Version => {\n            cmd_version();\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        CliCommand::TestAgent { name } => cmd_test_agent(&name),\n        CliCommand::Canary { status } => cmd_canary(status),\n        CliCommand::Upgrade { check } => cmd_upgrade(check),\n        CliCommand::Rollback => cmd_rollback(),\n        CliCommand::Reflect { workspace, force } => cmd_reflect(workspace, force),\n        CliCommand::UpdateRules { output } => cmd_update_rules(output),\n        CliCommand::Stats {\n            by,\n            since,\n            until,\n            format,\n        } => cmd_stats(by, since, until, format),\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Command handlers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// `needle run` — launch a worker.\n///\n/// Always creates dedicated tmux sessions for workers, even when invoked from\n/// inside an existing tmux session. The only exception is a re-entrant inner\n/// invocation launched by `launch_in_tmux()`, which is identified by the\n/// `NEEDLE_INNER=1` environment variable and runs the worker directly.\nfn cmd_run(\n    workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    agent: Option<String>,\n    count: u32,\n    identifier: Option<String>,\n    timeout: Option<u64>,\n    resume: bool,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    // Determine workspace root (CLI arg → canonicalized, else global default).\n    let workspace_root = if let Some(ref ws) = workspace {\n        ws.canonicalize().unwrap_or_else(|_| ws.clone())\n    } else {\n        let global = ConfigLoader::load_global()?;\n        global.workspace.default.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Load full resolved config (global → workspace .needle.yaml → env → CLI).\n    let cli_overrides = CliOverrides {\n        workspace: Some(workspace_root.clone()),\n        agent_binary: agent.clone(),\n        max_workers: None,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let (mut config, _) = ConfigLoader::load_resolved(&workspace_root, cli_overrides)?;\n\n    if let Some(t) = timeout {\n        config.agent.timeout = t;\n    }\n\n    if resume {\n        // Hot-reload resume: inherit worker identity from --identifier,\n        // load state from heartbeat file + registry, continue from SELECTING.\n        let worker_id = identifier\n            .clone()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| NATO_ALPHABET[0].to_string());\n\n        // Load resume state from heartbeat and registry.\n        let resume_state = crate::upgrade::ResumeState::load(&config, &worker_id)?;\n\n        // Emit upgrade.completed telemetry.\n        let current_hash = crate::upgrade::file_hash(\n            &std::env::current_exe().context(\"failed to locate own binary\")?,\n        )\n        .unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n\n        match &resume_state {\n            Some(state) => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    binary_hash = %&current_hash[..current_hash.len().min(12)],\n                    beads_processed = state.beads_processed,\n                    session = %state.session,\n                    \"resuming worker after hot-reload\"\n                );\n            }\n            None => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    binary_hash = %&current_hash[..current_hash.len().min(12)],\n                    \"resuming worker after hot-reload (no prior state found)\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        let tel = crate::telemetry::Telemetry::from_config(worker_id.clone(), &config.telemetry)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"hook telemetry init failed, falling back\");\n                crate::telemetry::Telemetry::new(worker_id.clone())\n            });\n        tel.emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::UpgradeCompleted {\n            new_hash: current_hash,\n        })?;\n\n        return run_worker(config, worker_id);\n    }\n\n    if is_needle_inner() {\n        // Re-entrant inner invocation launched by launch_in_tmux() — run\n        // the worker directly inside the dedicated session already created.\n        let worker_id = identifier\n            .clone()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| NATO_ALPHABET[0].to_string());\n        let agent_name = agent.as_deref().unwrap_or(&config.agent.default);\n        let session_name = format!(\"needle-{agent_name}-{worker_id}\");\n        tracing::info!(worker = %worker_id, session = %session_name, \"starting worker (inner re-entrant invocation)\");\n        run_worker(config, worker_id)\n    } else {\n        // Always create dedicated tmux sessions, even if already inside tmux.\n        launch_workers(config, workspace, agent, count, identifier, timeout)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Launch `count` workers in separate tmux sessions with staggered startup delays.\nfn launch_workers(\n    config: Config,\n    workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    agent: Option<String>,\n    count: u32,\n    identifier: Option<String>,\n    timeout: Option<u64>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let agent_name = agent\n        .as_deref()\n        .unwrap_or(&config.agent.default)\n        .to_string();\n    let stagger_secs = config.worker.launch_stagger_seconds;\n    let max_workers = config.worker.max_workers;\n\n    if count == 0 {\n        bail!(\"--count must be at least 1\");\n    }\n    if count as usize > NATO_ALPHABET.len() {\n        bail!(\n            \"--count {} exceeds the maximum of {} (NATO alphabet size)\",\n            count,\n            NATO_ALPHABET.len()\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Enforce max_workers cap (0 means unlimited).\n    let effective_count = if max_workers > 0 && count > max_workers {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            requested = count,\n            capped_to = max_workers,\n            \"count exceeds max_workers; capping\"\n        );\n        eprintln!(\n            \"Warning: --count {count} exceeds max_workers={max_workers}; launching {max_workers} workers\"\n        );\n        max_workers\n    } else {\n        count\n    };\n\n    // --identifier is only meaningful for a single worker.\n    if effective_count > 1 && identifier.is_some() {\n        bail!(\"--identifier cannot be combined with --count > 1; identifiers are auto-assigned from the NATO alphabet\");\n    }\n\n    // Detect existing sessions to avoid name collisions.\n    let occupied = occupied_worker_ids(&agent_name)?;\n    if !occupied.is_empty() {\n        tracing::info!(\n            occupied = ?occupied,\n            \"found existing worker sessions\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Reject --identifier collision early.\n    if let Some(ref id) = identifier {\n        if occupied.contains(id) {\n            bail!(\n                \"worker '{}' is already running in session 'needle-{}-{}'\",\n                id,\n                agent_name,\n                id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build the list of worker IDs, skipping occupied names.\n    let worker_ids: Vec<String> = if effective_count == 1 {\n        vec![identifier.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            // Pick the first available NATO name.\n            NATO_ALPHABET\n                .iter()\n                .find(|name| !occupied.contains(**name))\n                .map(|s| s.to_string())\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| NATO_ALPHABET[0].to_string())\n        })]\n    } else {\n        let mut ids = Vec::with_capacity(effective_count as usize);\n        for name in NATO_ALPHABET {\n            if ids.len() >= effective_count as usize {\n                break;\n            }\n            if occupied.contains(*name) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    worker_id = %name,\n                    \"skipping occupied worker name\"\n                );\n                continue;\n            }\n            ids.push(name.to_string());\n        }\n        if ids.len() < effective_count as usize {\n            bail!(\n                \"cannot launch {} workers — only {} NATO names available ({} occupied)\",\n                effective_count,\n                ids.len(),\n                occupied.len()\n            );\n        }\n        ids\n    };\n\n    for (seq, worker_id) in worker_ids.iter().enumerate() {\n        let session_name = format!(\"needle-{agent_name}-{worker_id}\");\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker_id = %worker_id,\n            sequence = seq,\n            total = effective_count,\n            session = %session_name,\n            \"launching worker\"\n        );\n\n        // Stagger: sleep before launching subsequent workers.\n        if seq > 0 && stagger_secs > 0 {\n            std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(stagger_secs));\n        }\n\n        launch_in_tmux(\n            &session_name,\n            workspace.clone(),\n            agent.clone(),\n            Some(worker_id.clone()),\n            timeout,\n        )?;\n\n        println!(\n            \"[{}/{}] Started worker '{}' in tmux session: {session_name}\",\n            seq + 1,\n            effective_count,\n            worker_id\n        );\n    }\n\n    if effective_count > 1 {\n        println!(\n            \"\\nStarted {effective_count} workers (stagger: {stagger_secs}s between launches).\"\n        );\n        println!(\"Attach to a worker with: tmux attach -t needle-{agent_name}-<name>\");\n    } else {\n        let worker_id = &worker_ids[0];\n        println!(\"Attach with: tmux attach -t needle-{agent_name}-{worker_id}\");\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Start the worker state machine (called when inside tmux or for direct mode).\n///\n/// Creates telemetry and the tokio runtime *before* any other initialization\n/// so that `worker.booting` is the very first JSONL event. Each subsequent\n/// init step is wrapped with `init.step.started` / `init.step.completed` so a\n/// silent hang pinpoints the exact blocking call.\nfn run_worker(config: Config, worker_name: String) -> Result<()> {\n    let boot_start = Instant::now();\n    let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n\n    // Phase 0: create tokio runtime + telemetry, emit worker.booting immediately.\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().context(\"failed to create tokio runtime\")?;\n    let telemetry =\n        Telemetry::from_config(qualified_id.clone(), &config.telemetry).unwrap_or_else(|e| {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to create hook-enabled telemetry, falling back\");\n            Telemetry::new(qualified_id.clone())\n        });\n    // Wait for the writer thread to be ready before emitting events.\n    // This fixes a race condition where emit() would send to the channel\n    // before the writer thread started processing, causing force_flush to timeout.\n    rt.block_on(telemetry.start_and_wait())\n        .context(\"writer thread failed to start\")?;\n    telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: worker_name.clone(),\n        version: env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string(),\n    })?;\n    // Force-flush worker.booting to disk immediately so we get a trace even if\n    // subsequent init steps block indefinitely (e.g., db lock, network call).\n    telemetry.force_flush(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))?;\n\n    // Phase 1: bead store discovery.\n    let store: Arc<dyn crate::bead_store::BeadStore> =\n        Arc::new(init_step(\"bead_store_discover\", &telemetry, || {\n            BrCliBeadStore::discover(config.workspace.default.clone())\n                .context(\"failed to locate br CLI for bead store\")\n        })?);\n\n    // Phase 2: worker construction (heavy — prompt loading, adapter discovery, etc.).\n    let mut worker = init_step(\"worker_construction\", &telemetry, || {\n        Ok(Worker::new_with_telemetry(\n            config,\n            worker_name.clone(),\n            store,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        ))\n    })?;\n\n    // Boot timeout guard: self-abort if init took >60 s.\n    let elapsed_ms = boot_start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n    if elapsed_ms > 60_000 {\n        telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { elapsed_ms })?;\n        bail!(\"boot exceeded 60 s ({elapsed_ms} ms), aborting\");\n    }\n\n    let result = rt.block_on(worker.run())?;\n\n    tracing::info!(final_state = %result, \"worker finished\");\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Emit start/complete telemetry around a fallible initialization step.\n///\n/// Each step's completion is force-flushed to disk so that if a subsequent\n/// step blocks indefinitely, the telemetry log shows exactly where it stopped.\nfn init_step<T, F>(name: &str, tel: &Telemetry, f: F) -> Result<T>\nwhere\n    F: FnOnce() -> Result<T>,\n{\n    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepStarted {\n        step: name.to_string(),\n    })?;\n    let t = Instant::now();\n    let result = f();\n    let elapsed = t.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n    tel.emit(EventKind::InitStepCompleted {\n        step: name.to_string(),\n        duration_ms: elapsed,\n    })?;\n    // Force-flush so the step completion is visible before the next (potentially blocking) step.\n    tel.force_flush(std::time::Duration::from_secs(1))?;\n    result\n}\n\n/// Returns true when this process is a re-entrant inner invocation launched\n/// by `launch_in_tmux()`, indicated by `NEEDLE_INNER=1` in the environment.\nfn is_needle_inner() -> bool {\n    std::env::var(\"NEEDLE_INNER\").is_ok_and(|v| v == \"1\")\n}\n\n/// Create a single tmux session and re-exec self inside it with `--count 1`.\nfn launch_in_tmux(\n    session_name: &str,\n    workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    agent: Option<String>,\n    identifier: Option<String>,\n    timeout: Option<u64>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    // Build the command that tmux will run inside the session.\n    let self_exe = std::env::current_exe().context(\"failed to locate own binary\")?;\n    let mut inner_args = vec![\"run\".to_string()];\n\n    if let Some(ref ws) = workspace {\n        inner_args.push(\"--workspace\".to_string());\n        inner_args.push(ws.display().to_string());\n    }\n    if let Some(ref a) = agent {\n        inner_args.push(\"--agent\".to_string());\n        inner_args.push(a.clone());\n    }\n    // Each session runs exactly one worker; identifier is always resolved before call.\n    inner_args.push(\"--count\".to_string());\n    inner_args.push(\"1\".to_string());\n    if let Some(ref id) = identifier {\n        inner_args.push(\"--identifier\".to_string());\n        inner_args.push(id.clone());\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = timeout {\n        inner_args.push(\"--timeout\".to_string());\n        inner_args.push(t.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Build stderr log path: ~/.needle/logs/<session_name>.stderr.log\n    let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \".\".to_string());\n    let stderr_log = format!(\"{home}/.needle/logs/{session_name}.stderr.log\");\n\n    let inner_cmd = format!(\n        \"NEEDLE_INNER=1 {} {} 2>> {}; tmux kill-session -t {}\",\n        shell_escape(&self_exe.display().to_string()),\n        inner_args\n            .iter()\n            .map(|a| shell_escape(a))\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\" \"),\n        shell_escape(&stderr_log),\n        shell_escape(session_name)\n    );\n\n    let status = ProcessCommand::new(\"tmux\")\n        .args([\"new-session\", \"-d\", \"-s\", session_name, &inner_cmd])\n        .status()\n        .context(\"failed to launch tmux — is tmux installed?\")?;\n\n    if !status.success() {\n        bail!(\n            \"tmux new-session exited with status {} for session '{}'\",\n            status,\n            session_name\n        );\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// `needle stop` — send SIGTERM to worker processes in tmux sessions.\nfn cmd_stop(all: bool, identifier: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n    if !all && identifier.is_none() {\n        bail!(\"specify --all or --identifier <NAME>\");\n    }\n\n    let sessions = list_needle_sessions()?;\n\n    if sessions.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"No needle sessions running.\");\n        return Ok(());\n    }\n\n    let targets: Vec<&str> = if all {\n        sessions.iter().map(|s| s.name.as_str()).collect()\n    } else {\n        let id = identifier.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n        sessions\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|s| s.name.contains(id))\n            .map(|s| s.name.as_str())\n            .collect()\n    };\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"No matching sessions found.\");\n        return Ok(());\n    }\n\n    for session in &targets {\n        tracing::info!(session = %session, \"sending SIGTERM to tmux session\");\n        // tmux send-keys sends C-c to the foreground process\n        let status = ProcessCommand::new(\"tmux\")\n            .args([\"send-keys\", \"-t\", session, \"C-c\", \"\"])\n            .status()\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to send SIGTERM to session '{session}'\"))?;\n\n        if status.success() {\n            println!(\"Stopped: {session}\");\n        } else {\n            println!(\"Warning: could not stop session '{session}' (status: {status})\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// `needle list` — show running needle sessions.\nfn cmd_list(format: ListFormat) -> Result<()> {\n    let sessions = list_needle_sessions()?;\n\n    if sessions.is_empty() {\n        match format {\n            ListFormat::Table => println!(\"No needle sessions running.\"),\n            ListFormat::Json => println!(\"[]\"),\n        }\n        return Ok(());\n    }\n\n    match format {\n        ListFormat::Table => {\n            println!(\"{:<40} {:<20} {:<10}\", \"SESSION\", \"CREATED\", \"STATUS\");\n            println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(70));\n            for s in &sessions {\n                println!(\"{:<40} {:<20} {:<10}\", s.name, s.created, s.status);\n            }\n        }\n        ListFormat::Json => {\n            let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&sessions)\n                .context(\"failed to serialize sessions to JSON\")?;\n            println!(\"{json}\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// `needle version` — print version info.\nfn cmd_version() {\n    let version = env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\");\n    let os = std::env::consts::OS;\n    let arch = std::env::consts::ARCH;\n    println!(\"needle {version} (rust, {os} {arch})\");\n}\n\n/// `needle test-agent <name>` — validate an agent adapter.\nfn cmd_test_agent(name: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = ConfigLoader::load_global()?;\n    let result = dispatch::test_agent(name, &config)?;\n    dispatch::print_test_result(&result);\n\n    if result.status == dispatch::AgentTestStatus::Error {\n        bail!(\"agent adapter '{}' is not ready\", name);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// attach, status, config, doctor\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// `needle attach <identifier>` — attach to a running worker's tmux session.\nfn cmd_attach(identifier: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let sessions = list_needle_sessions()?;\n\n    if sessions.is_empty() {\n        bail!(\"no needle sessions running\");\n    }\n\n    // Find matching session: exact match on identifier portion or substring match on full name.\n    let matches: Vec<&TmuxSession> = sessions\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|s| s.name.ends_with(&format!(\"-{identifier}\")) || s.name.contains(identifier))\n        .collect();\n\n    if matches.is_empty() {\n        let available: Vec<&str> = sessions.iter().map(|s| s.name.as_str()).collect();\n        bail!(\n            \"no session matching '{}'; available: {}\",\n            identifier,\n            available.join(\", \")\n        );\n    }\n\n    if matches.len() > 1 {\n        let names: Vec<&str> = matches.iter().map(|s| s.name.as_str()).collect();\n        bail!(\n            \"ambiguous identifier '{}'; matches: {}\",\n            identifier,\n            names.join(\", \")\n        );\n    }\n\n    let session = &matches[0].name;\n    let status = ProcessCommand::new(\"tmux\")\n        .args([\"attach-session\", \"-t\", session])\n        .status()\n        .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to attach to tmux session '{session}'\"))?;\n\n    if !status.success() {\n        bail!(\"tmux attach-session exited with status {status} for '{session}'\");\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// `needle status` — show fleet status summary.\nfn cmd_status(\n    format: ListFormat,\n    by_worker: bool,\n    cost: bool,\n    since: Option<String>,\n    until: Option<String>,\n    idle_strands: bool,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = ConfigLoader::load_global()?;\n    let needle_home = config.workspace.home.clone();\n    let registry = Registry::default_location(&needle_home);\n    let workers = registry.list().unwrap_or_default();\n    let sessions = list_needle_sessions().unwrap_or_default();\n\n    // Build a fleet summary.\n    let active_count = sessions.len();\n    let registered_count = workers.len();\n    let total_beads: u64 = workers.iter().map(|w| w.beads_processed).sum();\n\n    // Check heartbeat health for registered workers.\n    let heartbeat_dir = needle_home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n    let heartbeat_statuses: Vec<WorkerStatus> = workers\n        .iter()\n        .map(|w| {\n            let hb_path = heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", w.id));\n            let heartbeat = if hb_path.exists() {\n                std::fs::read_to_string(&hb_path)\n                    .ok()\n                    .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<HeartbeatData>(&s).ok())\n            } else {\n                None\n            };\n\n            let is_alive = is_pid_alive(w.pid);\n            let uptime = Utc::now().signed_duration_since(w.started_at);\n\n            WorkerStatus {\n                entry: w.clone(),\n                heartbeat_state: heartbeat.as_ref().map(|h| format!(\"{}\", h.state)),\n                current_bead: heartbeat.and_then(|h| h.current_bead.map(|b| b.to_string())),\n                pid_alive: is_alive,\n                uptime_secs: uptime.num_seconds().max(0) as u64,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    match format {\n        ListFormat::Table => {\n            println!(\"Fleet Summary\");\n            println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(50));\n            println!(\"  Active tmux sessions: {active_count}\");\n            println!(\"  Registered workers:   {registered_count}\");\n            println!(\"  Total beads processed: {total_beads}\");\n            println!();\n\n            if by_worker && !heartbeat_statuses.is_empty() {\n                println!(\n                    \"{:<16} {:<8} {:<14} {:<12} {:<10} {:<8}\",\n                    \"WORKER\", \"PID\", \"STATE\", \"BEAD\", \"UPTIME\", \"ALIVE\"\n                );\n                println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(68));\n                for ws in &heartbeat_statuses {\n                    let state = ws.heartbeat_state.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let bead = ws.current_bead.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"-\");\n                    let uptime = format_duration(ws.uptime_secs);\n                    let alive = if ws.pid_alive { \"yes\" } else { \"no\" };\n                    println!(\n                        \"{:<16} {:<8} {:<14} {:<12} {:<10} {:<8}\",\n                        ws.entry.id, ws.entry.pid, state, bead, uptime, alive\n                    );\n                }\n            } else if !heartbeat_statuses.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"Workers:\");\n                for ws in &heartbeat_statuses {\n                    let state = ws.heartbeat_state.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let alive = if ws.pid_alive { \"\" } else { \" (DEAD)\" };\n                    println!(\n                        \"  {} — {} beads, state: {state}{alive}\",\n                        ws.entry.id, ws.entry.beads_processed,\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n\n            if heartbeat_statuses.is_empty() && active_count == 0 {\n                println!(\"No workers running.\");\n            }\n        }\n        ListFormat::Json => {\n            let summary = serde_json::json!({\n                \"active_sessions\": active_count,\n                \"registered_workers\": registered_count,\n                \"total_beads_processed\": total_beads,\n                \"workers\": heartbeat_statuses.iter().map(|ws| {\n                    serde_json::json!({\n                        \"id\": ws.entry.id,\n                        \"pid\": ws.entry.pid,\n                        \"workspace\": ws.entry.workspace,\n                        \"agent\": ws.entry.agent,\n                        \"beads_processed\": ws.entry.beads_processed,\n                        \"state\": ws.heartbeat_state,\n                        \"current_bead\": ws.current_bead,\n                        \"pid_alive\": ws.pid_alive,\n                        \"uptime_secs\": ws.uptime_secs,\n                    })\n                }).collect::<Vec<_>>(),\n            });\n            println!(\n                \"{}\",\n                serde_json::to_string_pretty(&summary).context(\"failed to serialize status\")?\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Cost summary (if requested).\n    if cost {\n        let log_dir = needle_home.join(\"logs\");\n        let since_dt = since.as_deref().map(telemetry::parse_since).transpose()?;\n        let until_dt = until.as_deref().map(telemetry::parse_until).transpose()?;\n        let events = telemetry::read_logs(&log_dir, since_dt, until_dt, None)?;\n        let cs = telemetry::compute_cost_summary(&events);\n        let by_worker_costs = telemetry::compute_cost_by_worker(&events);\n        let by_workspace_costs = telemetry::compute_cost_by_workspace(&events);\n\n        match format {\n            ListFormat::Table => {\n                println!();\n                println!(\"Cost Summary\");\n                println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(50));\n                println!(\"  Dispatch events:  {}\", cs.total_events);\n                println!(\"  Total cost:       ${:.4}\", cs.total_cost_usd);\n                println!(\n                    \"  Tokens:           {} in / {} out\",\n                    cs.total_tokens_in, cs.total_tokens_out\n                );\n                println!(\n                    \"  Agent time:       {}\",\n                    telemetry::format_duration_ms_public(cs.total_elapsed_ms)\n                );\n\n                if !by_worker_costs.is_empty() {\n                    println!();\n                    println!(\"  Per Worker:\");\n                    println!(\n                        \"  {:<16} {:>8} {:>12} {:>14} {:>14}\",\n                        \"WORKER\", \"EVENTS\", \"COST (USD)\", \"TOKENS IN\", \"TOKENS OUT\"\n                    );\n                    println!(\"  {}\", \"-\".repeat(64));\n                    for w in &by_worker_costs {\n                        println!(\n                            \"  {:<16} {:>8} {:>12.4} {:>14} {:>14}\",\n                            w.worker_id,\n                            w.total_events,\n                            w.total_cost_usd,\n                            w.total_tokens_in,\n                            w.total_tokens_out,\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if !by_workspace_costs.is_empty() {\n                    println!();\n                    println!(\"  Per Workspace:\");\n                    println!(\n                        \"  {:<40} {:>8} {:>12} {:>14} {:>14}\",\n                        \"WORKSPACE\", \"EVENTS\", \"COST (USD)\", \"TOKENS IN\", \"TOKENS OUT\"\n                    );\n                    println!(\"  {}\", \"-\".repeat(88));\n                    for w in &by_workspace_costs {\n                        let ws_display = if w.workspace.len() > 38 {\n                            format!(\"...{}\", &w.workspace[w.workspace.len() - 35..])\n                        } else {\n                            w.workspace.clone()\n                        };\n                        println!(\n                            \"  {:<40} {:>8} {:>12.4} {:>14} {:>14}\",\n                            ws_display,\n                            w.total_events,\n                            w.total_cost_usd,\n                            w.total_tokens_in,\n                            w.total_tokens_out,\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            ListFormat::Json => {\n                let cost_json = serde_json::json!({\n                    \"dispatch_events\": cs.total_events,\n                    \"total_cost_usd\": cs.total_cost_usd,\n                    \"total_tokens_in\": cs.total_tokens_in,\n                    \"total_tokens_out\": cs.total_tokens_out,\n                    \"total_elapsed_ms\": cs.total_elapsed_ms,\n                    \"by_worker\": by_worker_costs.iter().map(|w| serde_json::json!({\n                        \"worker_id\": w.worker_id,\n                        \"total_events\": w.total_events,\n                        \"total_cost_usd\": w.total_cost_usd,\n                        \"total_tokens_in\": w.total_tokens_in,\n                        \"total_tokens_out\": w.total_tokens_out,\n                        \"total_elapsed_ms\": w.total_elapsed_ms,\n                    })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),\n                    \"by_workspace\": by_workspace_costs.iter().map(|w| serde_json::json!({\n                        \"workspace\": w.workspace,\n                        \"total_events\": w.total_events,\n                        \"total_cost_usd\": w.total_cost_usd,\n                        \"total_tokens_in\": w.total_tokens_in,\n                        \"total_tokens_out\": w.total_tokens_out,\n                        \"total_elapsed_ms\": w.total_elapsed_ms,\n                    })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),\n                });\n                println!(\n                    \"{}\",\n                    serde_json::to_string_pretty(&cost_json)\n                        .context(\"failed to serialize cost summary\")?\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Idle-strands cooldown summary (if requested).\n    if idle_strands {\n        let state_base = needle_home.join(\"state\");\n\n        // Collect unique workspaces: default + all registered worker workspaces.\n        let mut workspaces: Vec<PathBuf> = Vec::new();\n        workspaces.push(config.workspace.default.clone());\n        for w in &workers {\n            if !workspaces.contains(&w.workspace) {\n                workspaces.push(w.workspace.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        let rows = idle_strand_rows(&config, &state_base, &workspaces);\n\n        match format {\n            ListFormat::Table => {\n                println!();\n                println!(\"Idle Strand Cooldowns\");\n                println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(80));\n                println!(\n                    \"{:<10} {:<30} {:<12} {:<22} {:<12}\",\n                    \"STRAND\", \"WORKSPACE\", \"ENABLED\", \"LAST RUN\", \"STATUS\"\n                );\n                println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(80));\n                for row in &rows {\n                    let ws_display = if row.workspace.len() > 28 {\n                        format!(\"...{}\", &row.workspace[row.workspace.len() - 25..])\n                    } else {\n                        row.workspace.clone()\n                    };\n                    let last_run = row\n                        .last_run\n                        .map(|t| t.format(\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M\").to_string())\n                        .unwrap_or_else(|| \"never\".to_string());\n                    let enabled = if row.enabled { \"yes\" } else { \"no\" };\n                    println!(\n                        \"{:<10} {:<30} {:<12} {:<22} {:<12}\",\n                        row.strand, ws_display, enabled, last_run, row.status\n                    );\n                }\n                if rows.is_empty() {\n                    println!(\"  No idle strand state found.\");\n                }\n                println!();\n                println!(\n                    \"Cooldown hours: reflect={}, weave={}, pulse={}, unravel={}\",\n                    config.strands.reflect.cooldown_hours,\n                    config.strands.weave.cooldown_hours,\n                    config.strands.pulse.cooldown_hours,\n                    config.strands.unravel.cooldown_hours,\n                );\n            }\n            ListFormat::Json => {\n                let json_rows: Vec<_> = rows\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|r| {\n                        serde_json::json!({\n                            \"strand\": r.strand,\n                            \"workspace\": r.workspace,\n                            \"enabled\": r.enabled,\n                            \"last_run\": r.last_run,\n                            \"cooldown_hours\": r.cooldown_hours,\n                            \"status\": r.status,\n                        })\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n                let idle_json = serde_json::json!({\n                    \"idle_strands\": json_rows,\n                    \"cooldown_hours\": {\n                        \"reflect\": config.strands.reflect.cooldown_hours,\n                        \"weave\": config.strands.weave.cooldown_hours,\n                        \"pulse\": config.strands.pulse.cooldown_hours,\n                        \"unravel\": config.strands.unravel.cooldown_hours,\n                    }\n                });\n                println!(\n                    \"{}\",\n                    serde_json::to_string_pretty(&idle_json)\n                        .context(\"failed to serialize idle-strands\")?\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// `needle
